mission.title: k3s: Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster
mission.description: Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster. Requested by 36+ users.

steps.title: Check current k3s deployment
steps.description:
Verify your k3s version and configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes
```
This feature requires a working k3s installation.

steps.title: Review k3s configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
```
Currently, the parameter inputs (--cluster-cidr, --serice-cidr, etc) do not support more than one entry and that entry must be an IPv4 range.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
Problem: Although Kubernetes does support dual stack, K3S does not

Solution: Modify K3S so it can both support parameters for dual stack,
and pass them to the different Kubernetes daemons

#### Proposed Changes ####

Add dual stack support to K3S

#### Types of Changes ####

New Feature

####
```yaml
k3s server \
        '--tls-san' \
        '{{ k3s_master }}' \
        '--no-deploy' \
        'servicelb' \
        '--no-deploy' \
        'traefik' \
        '--docker' \
        '--flannel-backend=none' \
        '--default-local-storage-path=/dev/shm' \
        '--cluster-cidr=10.244.0.0/16,<RANGE>:c101:5810::/64' \
        '--service-cidr=10.43.0.0/16,<RANGE>:c101:5811::/64' \
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Problem: Although Kubernetes does support dual stack, K3S does not

Solution: Modify K3S so it can both support parameters for dual stack,
and pass them to the different Kubernetes daemons

#### Proposed Changes ####

Add dual stack support to K3S

#### Types of Changes ####

New Feature

#### Verification ####
You'll need to start K3S with the `DualStack` feature flag.
codeSnippets:
/usr/local/bin/k3s server --disable traefik –-flannel-backend=none --disable-network-policy  --disable servicelb --no-flannel --disable-kube-proxy  \
		--node-ip <node IPv4 IP> \
		--node-ip <node IPv6 IP> \
		--kube-apiserver-arg 'feature-gates=IPv6DualStack=true' \
		--kube-controller-manager-arg 'feature-gates=IPv6DualStack=true' \
		--kubelet-arg 'feature-gates=IPv6DualStack=true' \
		--cluster-cidr '<cluster IPv4 cidr>,<cluster IPv6 cidr>' \
		--service-cidr '<service IPv4 cidr>,<cluster IPv6 cidr>' \

# services "kube-dns" was not valid:
# * spec.ipFamilyPolicy: Invalid value: "RequireDualStack": Cluster is not configured for dual stack services

prerequisites.description: A running k3s cluster or a system where k3s can be installed.
